heading: Valuation of certain farm, etc., real property
label: 2032A Valuation of certain farm, etc., real property
notes: Inflation Adjusted Items for Certain YearsFor inflation adjustment of certain items in this section, see Revenue Procedures listed in a table under .section 1 of this title

References in TextThe Social Security Act, referred to in subsec. (b)(4)(A)(i), is , . Title II of the Social Security Act is classified generally to subchapter II (§ 401 et seq.) of chapter 7 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.act Aug. 14, 1935, ch. 53149 Stat. 620section 1305 of Title 42

L. 100–647, title VI, § 6151(b)102 Stat. 3724Nov. 10, 1988“(1).—In generalThe amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply with respect to rentals occurring after .December 31, 1976“(2).—Waiver of statute of limitationsIf on the date of the enactment of this Act [] (or at any time within 1 year after such date of enactment) refund or credit of any overpayment of tax resulting from the application of the amendment made by subsection (a) is barred by any law or rule of law, refund or credit of such overpayment shall, nevertheless, be made or allowed if claim therefore is filed before the date 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act.”Nov. 10, 1988

Effective Date of 1984 Amendment, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title X, § 1025(b)98 Stat. 1031Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095July 18, 1984Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—In generalThe amendment made by this section [amending this section] shall apply to estates of decedents dying after .December 31, 1976“(2).—Refund or credit of overpayment barred by statute of limitationsNotwithstanding section 6511(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] or any other period of limitation or lapse of time, a claim for credit or refund of overpayment of the tax imposed by such Code which arises by reason of this section may be filed by any person at any time within the 1-year period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act []. Sections 6511(b) and 6514 of such Code shall not apply to any claim for credit or refund filed under this subsection within such 1-year period.”July 18, 1984

L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Aug. 13, 1981Jan. 12, 1983Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—In generalExcept as otherwise provided in this subsection, the amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 1016, 1040, and 6324B of this title] shall apply with respect to the estates of decedents dying after .December 31, 1981“(2).—Increase in limitationThe amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply with respect to the estates of decedents dying after .December 31, 1980“(3)(d).—SubsectionThe amendments made by subsection (d) [amending this section and ] shall apply with respect to exchanges after .section 6324B of this titleDecember 31, 1981“(4)(e).—SubsectionThe amendments made by subsection (e) [amending this section] shall apply with respect to involuntary conversions after .December 31, 1981“(5)Certain amendments made retroactive to 1976.—“(A).—In generalThe amendments made by subsections (b)(1), (j)(1), and (j)(2) [amending this section and ] and the provisions of subparagraph (A) of section 2032A(c)(7) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (as added by subsection (c)(2)) shall apply with respect to the estates of decedents dying after .section 1040 of this titleDecember 31, 1976“(B).—Timely election requiredSubparagraph (A) shall only apply in the case of an estate if a timely election under section 2032A was made with respect to such estate. If the estate of any decedent would not qualify under section 2032A of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 but for the amendments described in subparagraph (A) and the time for making an election under section 2032A with respect to such estate would (but for this sentence) expire after , the time for making such election shall not expire before the close of .July 28, 1980February 16, 1982“(C).—Reinstatement of electionsIf any election under section 2032A was revoked before the date of the enactment of this Act [], such election may be reinstated at any time before .Aug. 13, 1981February 17, 1982“(D).—Statute of limitationsIf on the date of the enactment of this Act [] (or at any time before ) the making of a credit or refund of any overpayment of tax resulting from the amendments described in subparagraph (A) is barred by any law or rule of law, such credit or refund shall nevertheless be made if claim therefor is made before .”Aug. 13, 1981February 17, 1982February 17, 1982

L. 107–16, title V, § 581115 Stat. 93June 7, 2001“If on the date of the enactment of this Act [] (or at any time within 1 year after the date of the enactment) a refund or credit of any overpayment of tax resulting from the application of section 2032A(c)(7)(E) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is barred by any law or rule of law, the refund or credit of such overpayment shall, nevertheless, be made or allowed if claim therefor is filed before the date 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act.”June 7, 2001

Information Necessary for Valid Special Use Valuation Election, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 99–514, title XIV, § 1421100 Stat. 2716Pub. L. 100–647, title I, § 1014(f)102 Stat. 3562Oct. 22, 1986Nov. 10, 1988“(a).—In GeneralIn the case of any decedent dying before , if the executor—January 1, 1986“(1)made an election under section 2032A of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 [now 1986] on the return of tax imposed by section 2001 of such Code, and“(2)provided substantially all the information with respect to such election required on such return of tax,such election shall be a valid election for purposes of section 2032A of such Code.“(b).—Executor Must Provide InformationAn election described in subsection (a) shall not be valid if the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate after the date of the enactment of this Act [] requests information from the executor with respect to such election and the executor does not provide such information within 90 days of receipt of such request.Oct. 22, 1986“(c).—Effective DateThe provisions of this section shall not apply to the estate of any decedent if before the date of the enactment of this Act [] the statute of limitations has expired with respect to—Oct. 22, 1986“(1)the return of tax imposed by section 2001 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 [now 1986], and“(2)the period during which a claim for credit or refund may be timely filed.“(d).—Special Rule for Certain EstateNotwithstanding subsection (a)(2), the provisions of this section shall apply to the estate of an individual who died on , and with respect to which—January 30, 1984“(1)a Federal estate tax return was filed on , electing current use valuation, andOctober 30, 1984“(2)the agreement required under section 2032A was filed on .”November 9, 1984

Land Diverted Under 1983 Payment-in-Kind ProgramLand diverted from production of agricultural commodities under a 1983 payment-in-kind program to be treated, for purposes of this section, as used during the 1983 crop year by qualified taxpayers in the active conduct of the trade or business of farming, with qualified taxpayers who materially participate in the diversion and devotion to conservation uses under a 1983 payment-in-kind program to be treated as materially participating in the operation of such land during the 1983 crop year, see , set out as a note under .section 3 of Pub. Effective Date of 1981 Amendment, , , as amended by , , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 97–34, title IV, § 421(k)95 Stat. 313Pub. L. 97–448, title I, § 104(b)(4)96 Stat. 2382Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Aug. 13, 1981Jan. 12, 1983Oct. 22, 1986“(1).—In generalExcept as otherwise provided in this subsection, the amendments made by this section [amending this section and sections 1016, 1040, and 6324B of this title] shall apply with respect to the estates of decedents dying after .December 31, 1981“(2).—Increase in limitationThe amendment made by subsection (a) [amending this section] shall apply with respect to the estates of decedents dying after .December 31, 1980“(3)(d).—SubsectionThe amendments made by subsection (d) [amending this section and ] shall apply with respect to exchanges after .section 6324B of this titleDecember 31, 1981“(4)(e).—SubsectionThe amendments made by subsection (e) [amending this section] shall apply with respect to involuntary conversions after .December 31, 1981“(5)Certain amendments made retroactive to 1976.—“(A).—In generalThe amendments made by subsections (b)(1), (j)(1), and (j)(2) [amending this section and ] and the provisions of subparagraph (A) of section 2032A(c)(7) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (as added by subsection (c)(2)) shall apply with respect to the estates of decedents dying after .section 1040 of this titleDecember 31, 1976“(B).—Timely election requiredSubparagraph (A) shall only apply in the case of an estate if a timely election under section 2032A was made with respect to such estate. L. 100–647, title I, § 1014(f)102 Stat. 3562Oct. 22, 1986Nov. 10, 1988“(a).—In GeneralIn the case of any decedent dying before , if the executor—January 1, 1986“(1)made an election under section 2032A of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 [now 1986] on the return of tax imposed by section 2001 of such Code, and“(2)provided substantially all the information with respect to such election required on such return of tax,such election shall be a valid election for purposes of section 2032A of such Code.“(b).—Executor Must Provide InformationAn election described in subsection (a) shall not be valid if the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate after the date of the enactment of this Act [] requests information from the executor with respect to such election and the executor does not provide such information within 90 days of receipt of such request.Oct. 22, 1986“(c).—Effective DateThe provisions of this section shall not apply to the estate of any decedent if before the date of the enactment of this Act [] the statute of limitations has expired with respect to—Oct. 22, 1986“(1)the return of tax imposed by section 2001 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954 [now 1986], and“(2)the period during which a claim for credit or refund may be timely filed.“(d).—Special Rule for Certain EstateNotwithstanding subsection (a)(2), the provisions of this section shall apply to the estate of an individual who died on , and with respect to which—January 30, 1984“(1)a Federal estate tax return was filed on , electing current use valuation, andOctober 30, 1984“(2)the agreement required under section 2032A was filed on .”November 9, 1984

children.heading: Value based on use under which property qualifies
children.label: a Value based on use under which property qualifies
A
children.text: the decedent was (at the time of his death) a citizen or resident of the United States, and

children.label: B
children.text: the executor elects the application of this section and files the agreement referred to in subsection (d)(2),

children.heading: Limitation on aggregate reduction in fair market value
children.label: 2 Limitation on aggregate reduction in fair market value
children.text: The aggregate decrease in the value of qualified real property taken into account for purposes of this chapter which results from the application of paragraph (1) with respect to any decedent shall not exceed $750,000.

children.label: A
children.text: $750,000, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for such calendar year by substituting “calendar year 1997” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.label: i
children.text: on the date of the decedent’s death, was being used for a qualified use by the decedent or a member of the decedent’s family, and

children.label: ii
children.text: was acquired from or passed from the decedent to a qualified heir of the decedent.

children.label: B
children.text: 25 percent or more of the adjusted value of the gross estate consists of the adjusted value of real property which meets the requirements of subparagraphs (A)(ii) and (C),

children.label: i
children.text: such real property was owned by the decedent or a member of the decedent’s family and used for a qualified use by the decedent or a member of the decedent’s family, and

children.label: ii
children.text: there was material participation by the decedent or a member of the decedent’s family in the operation of the farm or other business, and

children.label: D
children.text: such real property is designated in the agreement referred to in subsection (d)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: use as a farm for farming purposes, or

children.label: B
children.text: use in a trade or business other than the trade or business of farming.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of the gross estate, the value of the gross estate for purposes of this chapter (determined without regard to this section), reduced by any amounts allowable as a deduction under paragraph (4) of section 2053(a), or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of any real or personal property, the value of such property for purposes of this chapter (determined without regard to this section), reduced by any amounts allowable as a deduction in respect of such property under paragraph (4) of section 2053(a).

children.heading: Decedents who are retired or disabled
children.label: 4 Decedents who are retired or disabled
i
children.text: was receiving old-age benefits under title II of the Social Security Act for a continuous period ending on such date, or

children.label: ii
children.text: was disabled for a continuous period ending on such date,

children.heading: Disabled defined
children.label: B Disabled defined
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), an individual shall be disabled if such individual has a mental or physical impairment which renders him unable to materially participate in the operation of the farm or other business.

children.heading: Coordination with recapture
children.label: C Coordination with recapture
children.text: For purposes of subsection (c)(6)(B)(i), if the requirements of paragraph (1)(C)(ii) are met with respect to any decedent by reason of subparagraph (A), the period ending on the date on which the continuous period taken into account under subparagraph (A) began shall be treated as the period immediately before the decedent’s death.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If property is qualified real property with respect to a decedent (hereinafter in this paragraph referred to as the “first decedent”) and such property was acquired from or passed from the first decedent to the surviving spouse of the first decedent, for purposes of applying this subsection and subsection (c) in the case of the estate of such surviving spouse, active management of the farm or other business by the surviving spouse shall be treated as material participation by such surviving spouse in the operation of such farm or business.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: B Special rule
children.text: For the purposes of subparagraph (A), the determination of whether property is qualified real property with respect to the first decedent shall be made without regard to subparagraph (D) of paragraph (1) and without regard to whether an election under this section was made.

children.heading: Coordination with paragraph (4)
children.label: C Coordination with paragraph (4)
children.text: In any case in which to do so will enable the requirements of paragraph (1)(C)(ii) to be met with respect to the surviving spouse, this subsection and subsection (c) shall be applied by taking into account any application of paragraph (4).

children.heading: Tax treatment of dispositions and failures to use for qualified use
children.label: c Tax treatment of dispositions and failures to use for qualified use
A
children.text: the qualified heir disposes of any interest in qualified real property (other than by a disposition to a member of his family), or

children.label: B
children.text: the qualified heir ceases to use for the qualified use the qualified real property which was acquired (or passed) from the decedent,

children.label: i
children.text: the adjusted tax difference attributable to such interest, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the excess of the amount realized with respect to the interest (or, in any case other than a sale or exchange at arm’s length, the fair market value of the interest) over the value of the interest determined under subsection (a).

children.heading: Adjusted tax difference attributable to interest
children.label: B Adjusted tax difference attributable to interest
i
children.text: the excess of the value of such interest for purposes of this chapter (determined without regard to subsection (a)) over the value of such interest determined under subsection (a), bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: a similar excess determined for all qualified real property.

children.heading: Adjusted tax difference with respect to the estate
children.label: C Adjusted tax difference with respect to the estate
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B), the term “adjusted tax difference with respect to the estate” means the excess of what would have been the estate tax liability but for subsection (a) over the estate tax liability. For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “estate tax liability” means the tax imposed by section 2001 reduced by the credits allowable against such tax.

children.label: i
children.text: the value determined under subsection (a) taken into account under subparagraph (A)(ii) with respect to such portion shall be its pro rata share of such value of such interest, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the adjusted tax difference attributable to the interest taken into account with respect to the transaction involving the second or any succeeding portion shall be reduced by the amount of the tax imposed by this subsection with respect to all prior transactions involving portions of such interest.

children.heading: Special rule for disposition of timber
children.label: E Special rule for disposition of timber
i
children.text: such disposition (or severance) shall be treated as a disposition of a portion of the interest of the qualified heir in such property, and

children.label: I
children.text: the amount realized on such disposition (or, in any case other than a sale or exchange at arm’s length, the fair market value of the portion of the interest disposed or severed), or

children.label: II
children.text: the amount of additional tax determined under this paragraph (without regard to this subparagraph) if the entire interest of the qualified heir in the qualified woodland had been disposed of, less the sum of the amount of the additional tax imposed with respect to all prior transactions involving such woodland to which this subparagraph applied.

children.heading: Only 1 additional tax imposed with respect to any 1 portion
children.label: 3 Only 1 additional tax imposed with respect to any 1 portion
children.text: In the case of an interest acquired from (or passing from) any decedent, if subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (1) applies to any portion of an interest, subparagraph (B) or (A), as the case may be, of paragraph (1) shall not apply with respect to the same portion of such interest.

children.heading: Due date
children.label: 4 Due date
children.text: The additional tax imposed by this subsection shall become due and payable on the day which is 6 months after the date of the disposition or cessation referred to in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Liability for tax; furnishing of bond
children.label: 5 Liability for tax; furnishing of bond
children.text: The qualified heir shall be personally liable for the additional tax imposed by this subsection with respect to his interest unless the heir has furnished bond which meets the requirements of subsection (e)(11).

children.label: A
children.text: such property ceases to be used for the qualified use set forth in subparagraph (A) or (B) of subsection (b)(2) under which the property qualified under subsection (b), or

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of periods during which the property was held by the decedent, there was no material participation by the decedent or any member of his family in the operation of the farm or other business, and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of periods during which the property was held by any qualified heir, there was no material participation by such qualified heir or any member of his family in the operation of the farm or other business.

children.heading: No tax if use begins within 2 years
children.label: A No tax if use begins within 2 years
i
children.text: no tax shall be imposed under paragraph (1) by reason of the failure by the qualified heir to so use such property before the commencement date, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the 10-year period under paragraph (1) shall be extended by the period after the decedent’s death and before the commencement date.

children.heading: Active management by eligible qualified heir treated as material participation
children.label: B Active management by eligible qualified heir treated as material participation
i
children.text: an eligible qualified heir, or

children.label: ii
children.text: a fiduciary of an eligible qualified heir described in clause (ii) or (iii) of subparagraph (C),

children.label: i
children.text: is the surviving spouse of the decedent,

children.label: ii
children.text: has not attained the age of 21,

children.label: iii
children.text: is disabled (within the meaning of subsection (b)(4)(B)), or

children.label: iv
children.text: is a student.

children.heading: Student
children.label: D Student
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (C), an individual shall be treated as a student with respect to periods during any calendar year if (and only if) such individual is a student (within the meaning of section 152(f)(2)) for such calendar year.

children.heading: Certain rents treated as qualified use
children.label: E Certain rents treated as qualified use
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, a surviving spouse or lineal descendant of the decedent shall not be treated as failing to use qualified real property in a qualified use solely because such spouse or descendant rents such property to a member of the family of such spouse or descendant on a net cash basis. For purposes of the preceding sentence, a legally adopted child of an individual shall be treated as the child of such individual by blood.

children.heading: Qualified conservation contribution is not a disposition
children.label: 8 Qualified conservation contribution is not a disposition
children.text: A qualified conservation contribution (as defined in section 170(h)) by gift or otherwise shall not be deemed a disposition under subsection (c)(1)(A).

children.heading: Election
children.label: 1 Election
children.text: The election under this section shall be made on the return of the tax imposed by section 2001. Such election shall be made in such manner as the Secretary shall by regulations prescribe. Such an election, once made, shall be irrevocable.

children.heading: Agreement
children.label: 2 Agreement
children.text: The agreement referred to in this paragraph is a written agreement signed by each person in being who has an interest (whether or not in possession) in any property designated in such agreement consenting to the application of subsection (c) with respect to such property.

children.heading: Modification of election and agreement to be permitted
children.label: 3 Modification of election and agreement to be permitted
A
children.text: the notice of election, as filed, does not contain all required information, or

children.label: B
children.text: signatures of 1 or more persons required to enter into the agreement described in paragraph (2) are not included on the agreement as filed, or the agreement does not contain all required information,

children.heading: Qualified heir
children.label: 1 Qualified heir
children.text: The term “qualified heir” means, with respect to any property, a member of the decedent’s family who acquired such property (or to whom such property passed) from the decedent. If a qualified heir disposes of any interest in qualified real property to any member of his family, such member shall thereafter be treated as the qualified heir with respect to such interest.

children.label: A
children.text: an ancestor of such individual,

children.label: B
children.text: the spouse of such individual,

children.label: C
children.text: a lineal descendant of such individual, of such individual’s spouse, or of a parent of such individual, or

children.label: D
children.text: the spouse of any lineal descendant described in subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Certain real property included
children.label: 3 Certain real property included
children.text: In the case of real property which meets the requirements of subparagraph (C) of subsection (b)(1), residential buildings and related improvements on such real property occupied on a regular basis by the owner or lessee of such real property or by persons employed by such owner or lessee for the purpose of operating or maintaining such real property, and roads, buildings, and other structures and improvements functionally related to the qualified use shall be treated as real property devoted to the qualified use.

children.heading: Farm
children.label: 4 Farm
children.text: The term “farm” includes stock, dairy, poultry, fruit, furbearing animal, and truck farms, plantations, ranches, nurseries, ranges, greenhouses or other similar structures used primarily for the raising of agricultural or horticultural commodities, and orchards and woodlands.

children.label: A
children.text: cultivating the soil or raising or harvesting any agricultural or horticultural commodity (including the raising, shearing, feeding, caring for, training, and management of animals) on a farm;

children.label: B
children.text: handling, drying, packing, grading, or storing on a farm any agricultural or horticultural commodity in its unmanufactured state, but only if the owner, tenant, or operator of the farm regularly produces more than one-half of the commodity so treated; and

children.label: i
children.text: the planting, cultivating, caring for, or cutting of trees, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the preparation (other than milling) of trees for market.

children.heading: Material participation
children.label: 6 Material participation
children.text: Material participation shall be determined in a manner similar to the manner used for purposes of paragraph (1) of section 1402(a) (relating to net earnings from self-employment).

children.label: i
children.text: the excess of the average annual gross cash rental for comparable land used for farming purposes and located in the locality of such farm over the average annual State and local real estate taxes for such comparable land, by

children.label: ii
children.text: the average annual effective interest rate for all new Federal Land Bank loans.

children.heading: Value based on net share rental in certain cases
children.label: B Value based on net share rental in certain cases

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: If there is no comparable land from which the average annual gross cash rental may be determined but there is comparable land from which the average net share rental may be determined, subparagraph (A)(i) shall be applied by substituting “average annual net share rental” for “average annual gross cash rental”.

children.label: I
children.text: the value of the produce received by the lessor of the land on which such produce is grown, over

children.label: II
children.text: the cash operating expenses of growing such produce which, under the lease, are paid by the lessor.

children.label: i
children.text: where it is established that there is no comparable land from which the average annual gross cash rental may be determined, or

children.label: ii
children.text: where the executor elects to have the value of the farm for farming purposes determined and that there is no comparable land from which the average net share rental may be determined under paragraph (8).

children.heading: Method of valuing closely held business interests, etc.
children.label: 8 Method of valuing closely held business interests, etc.
A
children.text: The capitalization of income which the property can be expected to yield for farming or closely held business purposes over a reasonable period of time under prudent management using traditional cropping patterns for the area, taking into account soil capacity, terrain configuration, and similar factors,

children.label: B
children.text: The capitalization of the fair rental value of the land for farm land or closely held business purposes,

children.label: C
children.text: Assessed land values in a State which provides a differential or use value assessment law for farmland or closely held business,

children.label: D
children.text: Comparable sales of other farm or closely held business land in the same geographical area far enough removed from a metropolitan or resort area so that nonagricultural use is not a significant factor in the sales price, and

children.label: E
children.text: Any other factor which fairly values the farm or closely held business value of the property.

children.label: A
children.text: such property is so considered under section 1014(b) (relating to basis of property acquired from a decedent),

children.label: B
children.text: such property is acquired by any person from the estate, or

children.label: C
children.text: such property is acquired by any person from a trust (to the extent such property is includible in the gross estate of the decedent).

children.heading: Community property
children.label: 10 Community property
children.text: If the decedent and his surviving spouse at any time held qualified real property as community property, the interest of the surviving spouse in such property shall be taken into account under this section to the extent necessary to provide a result under this section with respect to such property which is consistent with the result which would have obtained under this section if such property had not been community property.

children.heading: Bond in lieu of personal liability
children.label: 11 Bond in lieu of personal liability
children.text: If the qualified heir makes written application to the Secretary for determination of the maximum amount of the additional tax which may be imposed by subsection (c) with respect to the qualified heir’s interest, the Secretary (as soon as possible, and in any event within 1 year after the making of such application) shall notify the heir of such maximum amount. The qualified heir, on furnishing a bond in such amount and for such period as may be required, shall be discharged from personal liability for any additional tax imposed by subsection (c) and shall be entitled to a receipt or writing showing such discharge.

children.heading: Active management
children.label: 12 Active management
children.text: The term “active management” means the making of the management decisions of a business (other than the daily operating decisions).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any qualified woodland with respect to which the executor elects to have this subparagraph apply, trees growing on such woodland shall not be treated as a crop.

children.label: i
children.text: is used in timber operations, and

children.label: ii
children.text: is an identifiable area of land such as an acre or other area for which records are normally maintained in conducting timber operations.

children.label: i
children.text: the planting, cultivating, caring for, or cutting of trees, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the preparation (other than milling) of trees for market.

children.heading: Election
children.label: D Election
children.text: An election under subparagraph (A) shall be made on the return of the tax imposed by section 2001. Such election shall be made in such manner as the Secretary shall by regulations prescribe. Such an election, once made, shall be irrevocable.

children.heading: Treatment of replacement property acquired in section 1031 or 1033 transactions
children.label: 14 Treatment of replacement property acquired in section 1031 or 1033 transactions

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any qualified replacement property, any period during which there was ownership, qualified use, or material participation with respect to the replaced property by the decedent or any member of his family shall be treated as a period during which there was such ownership, use, or material participation (as the case may be) with respect to the qualified replacement property.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to the extent that the fair market value of the qualified replacement property (as of the date of its acquisition) exceeds the fair market value of the replaced property (as of the date of its disposition).

children.label: I
children.text: acquired in an exchange which qualifies under section 1031, or

children.label: II
children.text: the acquisition of which results in the nonrecognition of gain under section 1033.

children.label: I
children.text: the property transferred in the exchange which qualifies under section 1031, or

children.label: II
children.text: the property compulsorily or involuntarily converted (within the meaning of section 1033).

the statutory period for the assessment of any additional tax under subsection (c) attributable to such disposition or cessation shall not expire before the expiration of 3 years from the date the Secretary is notified (in such manner as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe) of such disposition or cessation (or if later in the case of an involuntary conversion or exchange to which subsection (h) or (i) applies, 3 years from the date the Secretary is notified of the replacement of the converted property or of an intention not to replace or of the exchange of property), and
such additional tax may be assessed before the expiration of such 3-year period notwithstanding the provisions of any other law or rule of law which would otherwise prevent such assessment.

children.heading: Application of this section and section 6324B to interests in partnerships, corporations, and trusts
children.label: g Application of this section and section 6324B to interests in partnerships, corporations, and trusts
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe regulations setting forth the application of this section and section 6324B in the case of an interest in a partnership, corporation, or trust which, with respect to the decedent, is an interest in a closely held business (within the meaning of paragraph (1) of section 6166(b)). For purposes of the preceding sentence, an interest in a discretionary trust all the beneficiaries of which are qualified heirs shall be treated as a present interest.

children.heading: Special rules for involuntary conversions of qualified real property
children.label: h Special rules for involuntary conversions of qualified real property
i
children.text: no tax shall be imposed by subsection (c) on such conversion if the cost of the qualified replacement property equals or exceeds the amount realized on such conversion, or

children.label: ii
children.text: if clause (i) does not apply, the amount of the tax imposed by subsection (c) on such conversion shall be the amount determined under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Amount of tax where there is not complete reinvestment
children.label: B Amount of tax where there is not complete reinvestment
i
children.text: bears the same ratio to such tax, as

children.label: ii
children.text: the cost of the qualified replacement property bears to the amount realized on the conversion.

children.label: A
children.text: any qualified replacement property shall be treated in the same manner as if it were a portion of the interest in qualified real property which was involuntarily converted; except that with respect to such qualified replacement property the 10-year period under paragraph (1) of subsection (c) shall be extended by any period, beyond the 2-year period referred to in section 1033(a)(2)(B)(i), during which the qualified heir was allowed to replace the qualified real property,

children.label: B
children.text: any tax imposed by subsection (c) on the involuntary conversion shall be treated as a tax imposed on a partial disposition, and

children.label: i
children.text: by not taking into account periods after the involuntary conversion and before the acquisition of the qualified replacement property, and

children.label: ii
children.text: by treating material participation with respect to the converted property as material participation with respect to the qualified replacement property.

children.heading: Involuntary conversion
children.label: A Involuntary conversion
children.text: The term “involuntary conversion” means a compulsory or involuntary conversion within the meaning of section 1033.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of an involuntary conversion described in section 1033(a)(1), any real property into which the qualified real property is converted, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of an involuntary conversion described in section 1033(a)(2), any real property purchased by the qualified heir during the period specified in section 1033(a)(2)(B) for purposes of replacing the qualified real property.

children.heading: Certain rules made applicable
children.label: 4 Certain rules made applicable
children.text: The rules of the last sentence of section 1033(a)(2)(A) shall apply for purposes of paragraph (3)(B)(ii).

children.heading: Exchanges of qualified real property
children.label: i Exchanges of qualified real property

children.heading: Exchanges solely for qualified exchange property
children.label: A Exchanges solely for qualified exchange property
children.text: If an interest in qualified real property is exchanged solely for an interest in qualified exchange property in a transaction which qualifies under section 1031, no tax shall be imposed by subsection (c) by reason of such exchange.

children.heading: Exchanges where other property received
children.label: B Exchanges where other property received
i
children.text: bears the same ratio to such tax, as

children.label: ii
children.text: the fair market value of the qualified exchange property bears to the fair market value of the qualified real property exchanged.

children.label: A
children.text: any interest in qualified exchange property shall be treated in the same manner as if it were a portion of the interest in qualified real property which was exchanged,

children.label: B
children.text: any tax imposed by subsection (c) by reason of the exchange shall be treated as a tax imposed on a partial disposition, and

children.label: C
children.text: paragraph (6) of subsection (c) shall be applied by treating material participation with respect to the exchanged property as material participation with respect to the qualified exchange property.

children.heading: Qualified exchange property
children.label: 3 Qualified exchange property
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “qualified exchange property” means real property which is to be used for the qualified use set forth in subparagraph (A) or (B) of subsection (b)(2) under which the real property exchanged therefor originally qualified under subsection (a).
